name: Benavente and La Polvorosa Valley Loop
name_original: Manganeses de la Polvorosa a Monte de Mózar — circular desde Benavente
description: This moderate 29.5-mile (47.5 km) mountain biking route from Benavente offers a circular journey through agricultural landscapes and open fi
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Benavente, Spain, traverse a landscape of rolling hills dotted with oak trees, serving as a transition zone between plains and mountains. This region features extensive networks of cross-country trails suitable for various skill levels. In Portugal, Benavente offers routes through fertile alluvial plains, river systems, and natural reserves near the Tagus Estuary. Both locations provide distinct natural features for mountain biking exploration. Best mountain bike trails around Benavente The most popular mountain bike trail is Santa Cristina de la Polvorosa a Peña Redonda — circular desde Benavente, a 35.0 miles (56.4 km) trail that takes 5 hours to complete. This difficult route offers a challenging circular journey through the local terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Vía de la Plata Greenway and Iron Bridge Loop, an easy 13.6 miles (21.9 km) path. This route follows a historic greenway, offering scenic views and a gentle ride. Local mountain bikers also love the Benavente and La Polvorosa Valley Loop, a 29.5 miles (47.5 km) trail leading through the La Polvorosa Valley, often completed in about 3 hours 33 minutes. Mountain biking around Benavente is defined by rolling hills, oak forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy greenways to more challenging climbs. The routes in Benavente are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Benavente's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Benavente?
A: The Benavente region offers diverse landscapes for mountain biking. In Spain, you'll find trails traversing rolling hills dotted with holm and Pyrenean oak trees, providing a transition between plains and mountains. In Portugal, the routes lead through fertile alluvial plains, river systems, and natural reserves near the Tagus Estuary, often following walking paths suitable for cycling.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Benavente?
A: Yes, Benavente offers several easy mountain bike trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1232563315/via-de-la-plata-greenway-and-iron-bridge-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía de la Plata Greenway and Iron Bridge Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 13.6-mile (21.9 km) path that follows a historic greenway, offering scenic views and a gentle ride perfect for those new to mountain biking. Overall, 4 of the 28 available tours are rated as easy.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes in the Benavente area?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Benavente has difficult routes available. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1230876731/santa-cristina-de-la-polvorosa-a-pena-redonda-circular-desde-benavente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Cristina de la Polvorosa a Peña Redonda — circular desde Benavente&lt;/a&gt;, a 35.0-mile (56.4 km) trail with significant elevation gain, typically taking around 5 hours to complete. There are 3 difficult routes in total.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Benavente?
A: Yes, many mountain bike trails in Benavente are designed as loops. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1232421845/benavente-and-la-polvorosa-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benavente and La Polvorosa Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 29.5-mile (47.5 km) route that takes you through the scenic La Polvorosa Valley, offering a complete circular journey.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: As you cycle, you can encounter several points of interest. Keep an eye out for historical bridges like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge of the Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2810181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Esla River&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422594" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de Azoague Iron Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass through the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2720898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;settlement of Benavente&lt;/a&gt; itself or see the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María del Azogue&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Benavente?
A: The Benavente region generally offers good conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, especially with the oak trees in Spain or the rich birdlife near the Tagus Estuary in Portugal. Summers can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are often preferred. Winters are generally mild enough for riding, though some trails might be muddy after rain.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that follow greenways or historic paths?
A: Yes, the region features trails that utilize historic infrastructure. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1232546515/via-verde-de-la-plata-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Plata Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering a moderate 21.6-mile (34.8 km) ride along a former railway line, providing a smooth and scenic experience.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Benavente?
A: The mountain bike trails around Benavente are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.0 stars from over 10 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from the rolling oak-covered hills of Spain to the serene river plains of Portugal, and appreciate the options available for different skill levels.

Q: Are e-bikes permitted on the mountain bike trails in Benavente?
A: Yes, e-bikes are generally permitted on most mountain bike trails in the Benavente region, allowing riders of varying fitness levels to enjoy the extensive network of routes.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while mountain biking in Benavente?
A: In the Spanish Benavente, you'll be riding through areas with recovering oak forests, which support various local wildlife. In the Portuguese Benavente, particularly near the Tagus Estuary Nature Reserve, you have excellent opportunities for wild birdlife observation, as it's a significant area for migratory birds.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Benavente region?
A: The Benavente region offers a substantial network for mountain biking, with a total of 28 distinct tours available. These range from easy rides to more challenging routes, catering to various preferences and skill levels.

Q: Are there any longer mountain bike routes for endurance riders?
A: For those looking for longer rides, the Benavente area provides several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17541737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Cristina de la Polvorosa – Barrero loop from Benavente&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 41.1-mile (66.2 km) route with significant elevation, making it suitable for endurance mountain bikers.
tips: Benavente is a good place to start or end a fun mountain bike adventure. If you have time you can also visit the museum in the old Casa Solita palace, the church of Santa María del Azogue and the Castillo de la Mota fortress.

Castle-fortress from the 12th century located on top of a hill overlooking the surrounding landscape. Since 1972 it has been owned by the Parador Nacional de Turismo group and functions as a vacation hotel.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate, requiring a good level of fitness. While the paths are generally accessible and not overly technical, it's best suited for riders with some experience rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect varied but generally manageable surfaces. The route likely features a mix of agricultural landscapes, open fields, and riverine areas typical of the Zamora region, offering a diverse riding experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: Since the route is circular and starts and ends near Benavente, you'll likely find parking options within or on the outskirts of Benavente. Specific parking areas are not detailed, but Benavente serves as a convenient starting point.

faqs.question: Are there public transport options to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Benavente is a regional hub, so public transport options like buses are generally available to reach the town. From Benavente, you would then access the starting point of the circular route.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 47.5 km long with a duration of around 3 hours and 33 minutes. This estimate is for riding time, so factor in additional time for breaks and enjoying the scenery.

faqs.question: What landmarks or points of interest will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: While the route itself offers scenic views, you'll find notable landmarks in the vicinity of Benavente. These include the Church of Santa María del Azogue and the Benavente Castle – Tower of the Snail.

faqs.question: Is the Manganeses de la Polvorosa a Monte de Mózar route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, mountain biking routes in agricultural and open areas are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through cultivated fields or near livestock. Be mindful of local regulations and other trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no special permits or entrance fees required to ride this mountain bike route. It's an open access trail.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Given the region's climate, spring and autumn are generally the best times to ride. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder, wetter conditions. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, Benavente offers various options for accommodation, cafes, and pubs. As the route is circular and accessible from Benavente, you can easily find places to refuel and relax before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Vía verde del oeste, Mtb Vía de la Plata, Vía de la Plata hiking - 03 Salamanca to Astorga, Camino de Santiago del Sureste, and Camino Mózar, among others.
